title: Athyrium malaccense
taxonomy: Athyrium malaccense is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is classified within the genus Athyrium and is a type of lady fern. The classification further breaks down to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Athyriaceae. The species name malaccense is derived from Malacca, a region in Malaysia, indicating its origin. This classification is based on the fern's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Athyrium malaccense is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant contains bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some regions, Athyrium malaccense is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. Further research is necessary to fully understand its potential medicinal applications and benefits.
